characters.name: Amazing Rope Guy
characters.description: Amazing Rope GuyGenderMaleOccupationVillainAlignmentEvil (Occasionally Neutral)RaceHumanAbilitiesTying knotsImpersonating voicesThrowing ropeMaking dioramasHyper-diggingVoice ActorLarry Murphy Impersonating voicesThrowing ropeMaking dioramasHyper-digging Amazing Rope Guyis a villain on the seriesWordGirl, voiced byLarry Murphy. He dresses in a tight dark blue mask, tight rubber dark blue outfit, tight yellow gloves, and tight yellow boots. He is not a serious bad guy as he is a fumbling comic-relief villain.

characters.name: Animal License Lady
characters.description: TheAnimal License Ladyis a woman who gives licenses for pets.  Her most known appearance was in the episodeBongo Rock, where Becky had come to her to get a license for Bob. She has made a few guest appearances in other episodes. Such asSuper-Grounded,Art in the Park, At the end ofWhere's Huggy?, she was seen backstage carrying a caged owl.

characters.name: Kumquat Anna
characters.description: Annais a 6 year old girl interviewed onWhat's Your Favorite Word?At that time her favorite word is "kumquat". Kumquat Annais thus a natural nickname for her. My name is Anna. My favorite word is "kumquat". My teacher thinks it's funny, and then she goes "look at my head," and then I started to like it.

characters.name: Anthony (reporter)
characters.description: Anthonyis a TV news anchorman on the Network News. He appeared as an in-studio TV announcer in many of the episodes, especially in the first two seasons. Anthony apparently was also a game show host at one time, since he appeared on the ballot in theMay I Have a Word?episode "Clutch". In a strange twist, a female version of Anthony appeared in "A Sticky Situation", anchoring "The Oddly Specific News."

characters.name: Artsy
characters.description: painting a bus Artsyis one ofTobey's robots that appeared in "Tobey's Masterpiece".  It was originally created for destruction, but proved to be much more artistic and creative than his other robots. It was first asked (or commanded) by Tobey to paint a picture of a bowl of fruit.  It also has somewhat of a personality, more so than Tobey's other robots.

characters.name: Beach Girl
characters.description: Thebeach girlappears in beach settings, mostly inCaptain Tangentepisodes, wearing a two tone yellow bikini. In "Silence of the Whams", she and another guy (likely her boyfriend) had their stuff taken from a scaredy catWhammer. Because of this, she leaves her boyfriend on purpose thinking that he let the Whammer have their beach stuff. "You're gonna let him take all of our stuff?!" - "Silence of the Whams"

characters.name: Beau Handsome
characters.actor_name: J.D. CernaTom Kenny(Tell Her What She's Won!)
characters.description: Beau HandsomeA/K/AMr. Handsome (as he is referred to by Emily and Becky)GenderMaleAgeAdultOccupationHost of "May I Have a Word?"HomelandPossiblyFair CityRaceHumanFriendsCaptain Huggyface/BobTommyEmilyPhilBelle Stunning(ex-girlfriend)Voice ActorJ.D. CernaTom Kenny(Tell Her What She's Won!) Beauregard "Beau" Handsomeis the host of the game showMay I Have a Word?which is shown after each episode. He has stated that his first name means "good-looking" in French. He is attracted to his own image. He has a brighter smile than his rivalSeymour Smooth. InDoze, he revealed that he has a pet cat when he was knitting her some earmuffs, which were too big for her. See gallery

characters.name: Beehive lady
characters.description: The phrasebeehive ladyrefers to an unnamed background character identifiable by her unique beehive hairstyle. She holds it up with a blue headband, wears a red shirt, and has a brown pearl necklace. In formal occasions, she wears a smaller white pearl necklace, a blue dress with an indigo bow for the waist and an indigo petticoat underneath. She looks similar toShelby Joy, and is sometimes seen along with her, implying that she may be Shelby Joy's mother, or a close relative. However, there is no confirmation of this within the series. She may be a member of high society or is just a regular citizen inSwap Meat, But it is revealed inChuck Makes a Buckthat she is a true member of the upper class despite that fact that she doesn't look or act like one.

characters.name: Belle Stunning
characters.description: Belle StunningisBeau Handsome's ex-girlfriend who is never seen but mentioned in theStrenuousepisode ofMay I Have a Word. Beau was attending a game show host reunion that Belle was attending. He had a set of weights and was doing bicep curls during the game show. When Tommy asked him what he was doing, he replied that he wanted Belle to see that he was still in good shape, implying that he still likes her.

characters.name: Benjamin J. Botsford
characters.description: Benjamin J. Botsfordis an ancestor thatTim Botsfordidentifies in a photo album inThe Ordinary, Extraordinary Botsfords. Tim says he worked on the very first car assembly line as a paint inspector.

characters.name: Billy
characters.description: Billyis a prince, and the cousin of princessVanilliin "Becky's Bad-itude".

characters.name: Bingo Announcer
characters.description: Bingo Announceris the woman who announces a number for bingo players.She fell for Granny May's trick, but goes back to WordGirl after Scoops publishes a story on how Granny May cheated. She even appeared before and after the episode. She also appears inInvasion of the Bunny Loversas a mind controlled victim.

characters.name: Blonde Haired Female Cop
characters.description: Blonde Haired Female CopGenderFemaleAgeAdultOccupationPolice OfficerAlignmentGoodHomelandFair CityRaceHuman TheBlonde Haired Female Copis a police officer in Fair City and is sometimes seen arresting villains afterWordGirlcaptures them. She wears a standard police uniform with blue pants and shirt with a blue police peaked hat, brown shoes, and a gold plated shield shaped badge on the right side of her shirt.

characters.name: Blue-dressed lady
characters.description: The phraseblue-dressed ladyrefers to an unnamed background character who appears in "Lunch Lady Chuck" as a passenger on the bus, "The Rise of Miss Power", and as a mind control victim of "Invasion of the Bunny Lovers". She also appeared as an audience member inPretty Princess Premiere. She has long blonde hair with some light streaks and it seen wearing a short sleeve blue dress,

characters.name: Blue-jacketed lady
characters.description: Blue-jacketed Lady The phraseblue-jacketed ladyrefers to an unnamed african american lady in the series seen wearing a blue jacket. She has dark skin, wears a blue jacket, black skirt, and hoop earrings. She is voiced by Cree Summer. She also is referred to asPanicking ladyas she appears in the opening sequence panicking and screaming at one ofTobey'sRobotswith thePointing lady,Running Man,Sally Botsford,Toque guy, andTodd "Scoops" Ming. She has a speaking role in "Down With Word Up".

characters.description: The phraseblue-shirted guyrefers to an unnamed background character seen throughout the series who wears a blue t-shirt with a folded-down neck collar on it. He has light brown hair and wears glasses. He seems to be the father ofCurious Benand married to theOak Street Lady. "A flying Skateboard!"- Mr Big's Big Plan. Give me a number

characters.name: Bogey
characters.description: BogeyisPatrick Needlemeyer's sidekick. He first appeared in "Putt With Honor".

characters.name: Bootsy
characters.description: Bootsy from "Mouse-Zilla" Bootsyis a minor character in the seriesWordGirl. He is an orange cat, with lighter orange spots, and wears a large pink bow and sunglasses. He is very famous, mainly for his lead role in the filmSir Kitty. Bootsy's first appearance was in the episode "Mousezilla", as the star of a hit film, andBeckyneeded to get his autograph for the City's scavenger hunt. Dr. Two Brains was terrified of him in the episode "Mouse-Zilla", and Tobey teased him about it.

characters.name: Bosco
characters.description: Bosco from her cameo in "Becky and the Bard". Boscois a small, brown-furred monkey, who (unlikeHuggy) has a tail. Her first appearance was as the monkey assistant ofProf. Tubingin "A Game of Cat and Mouse". She is also on a news team as well as working for Tubing. Her next, albeit cameo appearance, was in "Becky and the Bard". In said episode, she was Bob's date at a restaurant. This, combined with her more petite look and higher vocal range than Bob, gives the idea that she is, in fact, female.
